column5: After "Ved. to promote, favour", it should be a semicolon instead of comma
column6: (chiefly Ved.) to satisfy, refresh;  should be on a new line

column7: Typo – In the Pāṇinian system, “yaṇ” is a pratyāhāra (phonological abbreviatory term) encompassing the semivowels (y, v, r, l). It is absolutely never a secondary affix. The affix cited by APTE must be either “ṇya” for masculine definitions or “ṣyañ” for neuter definitions. The affix (ṣyañ) carries the sense of "the action or state of" (tasya karma bhāvo vā). Therefore, (kāvyam) is the objective, aesthetic creation. The affix (ṇya) carries the sense of "descendant of" (tasya apatyam). Therefore, (kāvyaḥ) is a living, masculine entity.

Typo – in standard Pāṇinian samāsa, the pūrvapada should be the uninflected stem: kumuda + ānanda; also, I am uncertain of the value of retaining the "hyphen" along with the "plus" as you have here: "+ -".   In fact you a retaining a useless "redundancy" !  The Hyphen itself is used to "imply" the addition of the final member to the previous member! Therefore the string of operations " + - ", that cologne maintains in the Window Display is a dynamic error, which deviates from the strict demands of logic!  Please correct this "Flaw" on the Global Level of AP Dictionary!
